answer:
\boxed{B}

Acetylcholine release depends on calcium influx through voltage-gated Ca²⁺ channels, which triggers synaptic vesicle fusion. Blocking these channels would prevent Ca²⁺ entry, reducing neurotransmitter release. The other options (Na⁺, K⁺, or Cl⁻ channels) do not directly regulate acetylcholine exocytosis.
